reasons: Diggie directly reduces Lolita's main teamfight threat by protecting allies from Noumenon Blast's crowd control.
Time Journey makes Lolita's charged engage less reliable when Diggie saves it for the real fight.

proof.summary: Lolita wants to win teamfights with Noumenon Blast's AoE stun, while Diggie can protect nearby allies with control immunity and shield during the engage window.
worksBestWhen: Diggie saves Time Journey for Noumenon Blast.
Allies stay close enough to receive Diggie's protection.
failureCases: Lolita baits Time Journey before the real engage.
Diggie is zoned or killed before Lolita channels.

reasons: Esmeralda can pressure shield-heavy frontlines because her kit interacts strongly with shields.
Lolita's shield-based protection loses value when Esmeralda reaches her or shielded allies.

proof.summary: Lolita wants to protect herself and allies with shield value, while Esmeralda can ignore shield effects and convert shield interaction into her own durability.
worksBestWhen: Esmeralda reaches Lolita or shielded allies.
The fight lasts long enough for Esmeralda to cycle skills.
failureCases: Lolita keeps Esmeralda away with team crowd control.
Esmeralda dives alone into layered control.

reasons: Franco is a conditional counter because Iron Hook is not stopped by Lolita's normal projectile-reflection plan.
Suppression can lock Lolita before she protects allies or finishes a strong Noumenon Blast setup.

proof.summary: Lolita wants to block ranged pressure with Guardian's Reflection, but Franco can threaten pickoff with hook and suppression if he times the engage correctly.
worksBestWhen: Franco hooks Lolita before she sets up a full teamfight.
Franco has team follow-up after hook or suppression.
failureCases: Franco misses hook and gives Lolita time to counter-engage.
Franco hooks a tanky Lolita without follow-up damage.

reasons: Kaja can suppress and drag Lolita out of position before she controls the fight.
This is useful when Lolita is acting as the main frontline or shield anchor.

proof.summary: Lolita wants to stand between her team and incoming damage, while Kaja can suppress and drag her out of position before she controls the fight.
worksBestWhen: Kaja reaches Lolita before she finishes charging Noumenon Blast.
Kaja's team can burst or displace Lolita after the drag.
failureCases: Lolita's team immediately counter-engages after Kaja commits.

reasons: Valir can make Lolita's approach harder with knockback and slow.
This does not directly counter every part of Lolita's shield, but it can deny her positioning before she reaches a strong Noumenon Blast angle.

proof.summary: Lolita wants to walk forward, protect allies, and find an AoE stun angle, while Valir can push her away and slow her before she reaches the backline.
worksBestWhen: Valir saves knockback for Lolita's forward movement.
Valir stays outside Lolita's engage angle.
failureCases: Lolita engages from fog or with a sudden angle before Valir reacts.
Valir wastes knockback on another target.
